title: Force "localhost" as a default registry
body: Extend `util.ResolveName()` to prepend "localhost" to the list of registries, and teach `util.FindImage()`, `util.ExpandNames()`, and `util.AddImageNames()` to use `util.ResolveName()`.

comments.body: BTW I believe this is the last patch we need to get in so we can declare Buildah 1.0  :1st_place_medal:

Note that when we're given an image name that doesn't include a registry component, we'll attempt to pull from a registry running on localhost first, before consulting registries listed in the configuration.  If the configuration doesn't exist, then, we'll just fail to pull an image.

We'll need to document the registry on the localhost if it doesn't work like the multituedd of Docker examples.  There are a number of examples for running a Docker registry on the web, will Buildah no longer talk to those registries without adjustments?  @ipbabble also  wrote a blog on how to set up a local and we'll need to make sure that holds or add an addendum to that blog.

There are several fixes in this patch suite to make sure that buildah uses the registries.conf properly.  So yes this change will not effect what is going on now, unless you don't have a properly populated registries.conf.  But I have a feeling our previous code was pulling from docker.io even if docker.io was not listed in the registry.conf.  Which would be a big bug.
Added some notes about completion of image names to the man pages.

@TomSweeneyRedHat This should work just fine with a localhost registry.  The only change would be if I do 
```
ctr=buildah from scratch
buildah commit $ctr foobar
```
I will end up with localhost/foobar as my image name.

If I had the same named package in a localhost registry and did a 

```
buildah from foobar
````
Then the original localhost/foobar would be replaced by the one pulled down.

This should not effect pushing, since you need to specify a path to push to.

@rh-atomic-bot r+

I agree I think we need to sprinkle information in all of the functions that do pull or push.

:pushpin: Commit d3cd79d has been approved by `rhatdan`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=rhatdan d3cd79d7c6c506acef4f6f50c69e381df86fb4b6 -->

This really confused me a lot as I didn't understand why my container build scripts weren't picking up changes, turned out of course they were running the `docker.io/` version - looks like this change landed in the Silverblue release stream recently.

Eh, yeah I guess it's better than doing `docker.io` anyways.
Sorry about that, tough to change the existing databases.
